client_preference_bias_note: Reviewer has read prior session context. Partial confirmation bias mitigated by: (a) every objection has a falsifiable kill-condition; (b) the v2 re-extraction itself was prompted by user challenge of facts, not by alignment with prior narrative.
v2_change_summary: v2 V5 design has fewer untested elements than v1 V5 (1 vs 3) because the matrix re-extraction revealed V4 already had refund copy, SEBI badge, aggregate metrics, crown branding. Many v1 blockers no longer apply.

objections.title: 'See 3 real trades, free' must actually deliver 3 free trades pre-payment
objections.challenge: If user taps the outline CTA and gets a paywall instead of 3 real trades, the copy is deceptive — bigger trust violation than V4's mismatched but vague labels. v1 raised this same concern; v2 sharpens to a precondition.
objections.kill_condition: Instrumentation event v5_payment_required_before_trade fires at all → stop-ship. v5_trade_shown_before_payment must fire on every outline-CTA tap.
objections.status_in_v2: Unresolved at design-time; must be operational commitment (Operational Precondition 1 in spec).

objections.title: 'Refund in 60s to source. No questions.' must match Univest's actual refund SLA per payment method
objections.challenge: If UPI is 30s but card is 5 days, the SLA is fiction. Skeptical Investor's trust gap closes only if the SLA is real. Same as v1; spec's per-payment-method SLA matrix in §4 addresses this.
objections.kill_condition: v5_refund_issued p90 elapsed_seconds > stated SLA for ≥ 1 week → switch to weaker copy ('Refund issued same business day') and update spec.
objections.status_in_v2: Unresolved at design-time; must be operational commitment with per-payment-method SLA matrix (Operational Precondition 2).

objections.title: Real closed trade risks cherry-picking; partly mitigated by wins/losses disclosure but trade selection rules still need spec
objections.challenge: Showing one closed winning trade reads as cherry-picked unless: (a) the user can browse all recent trades including losses, AND (b) the selection rule is published. v2 V5 helps via wins_losses_disclosure ('914 wins · 62 losses') which structurally defeats the 'they only show winners' suspicion at the aggregate level. But the specific shown trade still needs a defensible selection rule.
objections.kill_condition: If post-test 'browse all trades' link engagement < 5%, the cherry-picking mitigation didn't reach users. Switch to a 'mixed pool' display (1 win + 1 loss) for V5.1.
objections.status_in_v2: Mitigated structurally; spec must specify (i) trade selection rule (e.g., 'most recent closed trade in subscriber-tier portfolio, last 24h'), (ii) Browse all link visibility, (iii) max-trade-age threshold.

objections.title: Crown branding evidence
objections.challenge: v1 V5 had branding=none with weak evidence. v2 confirms V4 actually has crown_header (V2/V3/V4 all do). Crown is universal-adoption tier — well-evidenced.
objections.status_in_v2: RESOLVED by re-extraction. V5 keeps crown_header per V4.

objections.title: Trust Seeker green-CTA penalty
objections.challenge: V2->V3 contrast showed Trust Seeker -10pt from green CTA on light theme. V4 demonstrated recovery via dark-theme-restoration. v2 V5 keeps V4's dark + green combo, so the V3-light-theme penalty doesn't apply structurally. Residual risk: if Trust Seeker conversion still drops, switch to muted_premium dark teal (mockup pre-rendered as v5b-muted-premium.png).
objections.kill_condition: Trust Seeker < 47% after 2 weeks → switch to muted_premium for next ramp stage.
objections.status_in_v2: Watch item; post-ship contingency in place. Not a launch blocker.

objections.title: Removing countdown timer is a Bargain Hunter risk
objections.challenge: v1 matrix incorrectly had the timer as V1-only. v2 reveals timer is V1-V4. Removing it in V5 is a real change. Bargain Hunter conversion in V1 (54%) was 16pt above Control's 38% — some of that lift may have been timer-driven urgency. V4's 7s avg conversion suggests the structural urgency (price + 'free trades' framing) works without explicit timer, but the mechanism is partially confounded.
objections.kill_condition: Bargain Hunter conversion drops > 5pt vs V4 over 2 weeks → restore timer (V5.1 with timer = pure A/B test of timer effect).
objections.status_in_v2: Should-fix surfaced by re-extraction. Watch in instrumentation.

objections.title: Skeptical mechanisms are coupled — apply 0.7× discount per coupled mechanism
objections.challenge: 5 V5 levers target Skeptical via shared 'honesty substrate' (real trade + wins/losses + explicit SLA + countdown removal + CTA copy fix). Independent-mechanism assumption is wrong. Synthesize already applies 0.7 discount to the predicted point; estimator must propagate to interval.
objections.kill_condition: Skeptical observed lift < 50% of predicted point → coupling discount was insufficient; revise to 0.5 for next client.
objections.status_in_v2: Acknowledged in synthesis drivers list; estimator applies.

objections.title: Curious Beginner anchor depends on stock selection — out-of-matrix risk
objections.challenge: V1's named-wins effect (TMPV/ZOMATO/RELIANCE +rupee gains) depends on user pre-existing relationship with those specific stocks. V5's carousel will name different stocks. The 'I bought it last year' mechanism may not transfer if Univest's actual stock picks don't resonate with each user.
objections.kill_condition: Curious Beginner conversion < 30% after 2 weeks AND post-test 'named stock recall' < 10pt above pre-test → carousel personalization is required.
objections.status_in_v2: Should-fix; carousel personalization is a V5.1 upgrade.

summary.v2_summary_one_line: v2 V5 has zero design-blockers. The two remaining gates are operational commitments Univest must make (refund SLA per payment method, free flow delivers 3 trades pre-payment). Everything else is post-ship watch.
